vignette: A 22-year-old male presents with severe unilateral scrotal pain of sudden onset 1 hour ago, worse with elevation of the testicle. The testicle is high-riding and tender. Cremasteric reflex absent on the left.
reference: Surgical emergency — requires urgent exploration within 6 hours

vignette: A 5-year-old girl presents with sudden onset of inspiratory stridor, drooling, and sitting in a tripod position. Temperature 39.8°C. She appears toxic and anxious. Immunisation status unknown.
reference: Paediatric emergency — do not examine throat; immediate senior airway support

vignette: A 60-year-old woman presents with right upper quadrant pain, fever of 38.9°C, and jaundice over 3 days. She has a history of gallstones. WBC 18 x10⁹/L. Bilirubin 78 µmol/L. ALP elevated 3x upper limit of normal.
reference: Charcot's triad: RUQ pain + fever + jaundice. Tokyo Guidelines 2018.

vignette: A 35-year-old man is brought in by ambulance after a witnessed tonic-clonic seizure lasting 4 minutes. He is postictal on arrival. This is his first seizure. Blood glucose 3.1 mmol/L. Temperature normal. No focal neurology after 20 minutes.
reference: Blood glucose 3.1 mmol/L is the precipitant. Treat hypoglycaemia first.

vignette: A 55-year-old male smoker with COPD presents with 3 days of worsening dyspnoea and increased purulent sputum. Temperature 37.9°C. SpO2 88% on room air. CXR: right lower lobe consolidation. WBC 14 x10⁹/L. CRP 120 mg/L.
A 40-year-old woman presents with fatigue and weight gain. She takes no regular medications. Examination is unremarkable.
reason: Critically insufficient — no bloods, no duration, no associated symptoms, no vitals. Hypothyroidism, depression, diabetes, and many other conditions cannot be distinguished.

vignette: A 65-year-old man presents with chest pain. He has a past medical history of hypertension. He takes ramipril.
reason: No character, duration, radiation, associated symptoms, ECG, or troponin. Cannot exclude ACS, aortic dissection, PE, or musculoskeletal cause.

vignette: A 25-year-old woman presents with headache. The headache has been present for 2 days. She rates the pain 7/10.
reason: No character, location, associated features (fever, photophobia, neck stiffness, visual changes, thunderclap onset), or neurological exam. Cannot exclude SAH or meningitis.

vignette: A 50-year-old man presents with abdominal pain and has had some blood in his stool. He is otherwise well.
reason: No character of pain, location, duration, associated features, examination, or investigation results. Cannot distinguish IBD, colorectal cancer, haemorrhoids, or other causes.

vignette: A child is brought in by parents who are concerned about their behaviour. The child is 8 years old.
reason: Entirely non-specific. No description of behaviour, duration, developmental history, school performance, family context, or examination.
